authors: Amirdelfan K, Yu C, Doust MW, Gliner BE, Morgan DM, Kapural L, Vallejo R, Sitzman BT, Yearwood TL, Bundschu R, Yang T, Benyamin R, Burgher AH, Brooks ES, Powell AA, Subbaroyan J
title: Long-term quality of life improvement for chronic intractable back and leg pain patients using spinal cord stimulation: 12-month results from the SENZA-RCT
study_question: Does 10 kHz SCS improve quality of life more than traditional SCS in chronic back and leg pain?
population_assessed: Adults with chronic back and leg pain randomized at 10 US pain centers
outcome_measures: ODI, GAF, SF-MPQ-2, PSQI, SF-12, GIC
inclusion_criteria: Chronic back and leg pain; successful trial required permanent implant
answer_to_study_question: 10 kHz SCS improved disability, functioning, sleep, and pain measures more than traditional SCS at 12 months.
